title: Product Catalog — Array Storage
description: Replace single-product handling with arrays. Declare a string array for product names, a double array for prices, and an int array for quantities (max 100 products). Use an int counter to track how many products have been added. Loop through the arrays to display all products. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'names[' in cpp or 'name[' in cpp or 'productNames[' in cpp, 'Declare a string array for product names'
assert 'prices[' in cpp or 'price[' in cpp or 'productPrices[' in cpp, 'Declare a double array for product prices'
assert 'quantities[' in cpp or 'quantity[' in cpp or 'qty[' in cpp or 'productQty[' in cpp, 'Declare an int array for product quantities'
assert 'for' in cpp, 'Use a for loop to iterate through the arrays'
assert 'productCount' in cpp or 'numProducts' in cpp or 'count' in cpp.lower(), 'Track the number of products with a counter variable'
assert 'MAX_PRODUCTS' in cpp or 'MAX_SIZE' in cpp or 'CAPACITY' in cpp or '100' in cpp, 'Define a maximum array size'
acceptance_criteria: Declares parallel arrays: string names, double prices, int quantities
Uses a constant for maximum array size (e.g., MAX_PRODUCTS = 100)
Tracks number of products with a counter variable
Adds new products to arrays at the counter index
Uses a for loop to display all stored products
Saves all products to file using a loop
hints: Parallel arrays store related data at the same index: names[i] goes with prices[i] and quantities[i]
Always check productCount < MAX_PRODUCTS before adding a new product
Use a for loop: for (int i = 0; i < productCount; i++) to display all products
Increment productCount++ after successfully adding a product
